name: ROI Framing
summary: A persuasion technique that positions the decision around return, payoff, or business value rather than around price in isolation.
whenToUseIt: Use ROI framing when the buyer or CFO needs the investment justified in measurable terms and when you need to defend price against status quo or competitor pressure.
steps: Clarify the cost of the problem or the upside of solving it.
Connect your solution to a measurable change in revenue, efficiency, cost, or risk.
Frame the investment against the size and speed of the expected return.
example: A buyer hesitates at a software price until the rep frames it against the labor cost, error reduction, and deal-speed improvement expected over the next twelve months.
